steps.title: Write the equation of line k
steps.content: Use slope-intercept form with slope $m = 6$ and $y$-intercept $b = -48$. This gives: $$y = 6x - 48$$

steps.title: Find the x-intercept by setting y = 0
steps.content:
At the $x$-intercept, $y = 0$. Substitute into the equation: $$0 = 6x - 48$$
Add 48 to both sides: $$6x = 48$$
Divide both sides by 6: $$x = 8$$
The $x$-coordinate of the $x$-intercept is 8.
